mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving positive equity returns as corporate earnings expectations rise over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of observations or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which suppresses corporate profit margins and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods when INDPRO declines for two consecutive months during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, elevated discount rates and tighter financial conditions compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year window or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increases in new housing starts signal expanding residential investment and broader economic momentum, which translates into higher corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of periods following housing starts increases or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production above the 2017 baseline level indicates resolving supply chain bottlenecks and improving factory output, which reduces input cost pressures and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO crosses above 100 during post-shock recovery phases.
